name: Four States Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, CDs, loans, and digital banking with emphasis on personalized service.
description_long:
Four States Bank positions itself as a community-focused financial institution that prioritizes relationship-based banking over transactional models. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection up to standard limits. Based on their website content, they have multiple physical locations and maintain a local presence in their service areas.

Four States Bank offers a comprehensive range of banking products including personal checking accounts, business checking accounts, savings accounts, and Certificate of Deposit (CD) products. They advertise a 7-month CD special at 4.17% APY with a $1,000 minimum deposit. Their lending products span personal loans, SBA loans for small businesses, agricultural/farm loans, home loans, and home equity lines of credit (HELOC). They also provide digital banking capabilities including remote check deposits, digital loan transfers, and PDF transaction records.

The bank differentiates itself through an emphasis on personalized banking relationships rather than purely digital interactions. Their marketing highlights that customers are "known by name" and that they use technology as a tool to enhance personalized service rather than replace human interaction. They position themselves as community-invested, with language emphasizing that "when our clients thrive, the community blossoms" and that they understand individual customer goals and dreams.

Based on available website information, specific details about fees, loan terms, APR ranges, account minimums beyond the CD product, and current rates on other products are not disclosed. The website mentions a contact number (417-851-0568) for the CD special and encourages customers to visit branch locations, but comprehensive product details and competitive rate comparisons are not publicly available on the provided web content.
services: Online banking with PDF transaction records
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Competitive 7-month CD rate of 4.17% APY advertised on homepage
Multiple physical branch locations for in-person service
Digital banking features including remote check deposit and digital transfers
Diverse loan products (SBA, agricultural, home, business, personal)
Emphasis on personalized service and community relationship banking
Online banking portal with account management tools
cons: Limited rate and product details available online; requires phone call or branch visit for most information
No published information about checking account APY, fees, or minimum balance requirements
Geographic limitation appears to be regional (Four States area); service area not clearly defined online
No mention of mobile app capabilities or advanced digital features compared to larger banks
Loan rates, terms, and approval criteria not disclosed on website
best_for: Community-focused customers who prefer relationship banking and personal service over purely digital experiences
Small business owners and farmers seeking specialized lending products like SBA and agricultural loans
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance safety and local banking presence over online-only convenience
diagnosis: Four States Bank is best suited for customers in their service region seeking FDIC-insured banking with community-focused personal service and diverse lending options, particularly small business owners and agricultural operators. The main caveat is that specific product rates, fees, and terms are not available online, requiring direct contact with the bank, and service area appears geographically limited to the Four States region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
